name: Date Rolls
recipeIngredient: 3 cups whole wheat pastry flour (up to 3 1/2)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the sucanat, salt, and yeast with 1 cup of whole wheat flour and 1 cup of whole wheat pastry flour. Set aside.
2. In a saucepan, slowly heat water, margarine, and molasses/honey until the margarine melts.
3. Add the liquid to the dry ingredients and mix well. Gradually add the remaining flour and knead until firm but slightly sticky.
4. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm, draft-free place for one hour.
5. Meanwhile, cut pitted dates into small pieces, add water, and simmer until it becomes a soft, thick date butter. Cool.
6. When the bread dough has risen, divide it into two parts. Roll each part into a 1/4 inch rectangle and spread with date butter. Sprinkle with nuts.
7. Roll up each piece to form logs, then place on oiled baking sheets. Flatten and shape the rolls.
8. Place the sheets in a warm, draft-free place for 45 minutes.
9. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Bake the rolls for 15-20 minutes until lightly golden.
10. Optional: When cool, drizzle with maple topping (mix ingredients in a saucepan and heat just to a boil, then cool before drizzling on the rolls). Enjoy.
Maple Topping:
- Mix water, pure maple syrup, salt, cornstarch, vanilla, and maple flavoring in a saucepan. Heat until boiling, then let cool before drizzling over the rolls.
